title: Once in a Saintly Passion
content:
Once in a saintly passion
    I cried with desperate grief,
    "O Lord, my heart is black with guile,
    Of sinners I am chief."
    Then stooped my guardian angel
    And whispered from behind,
    "Vanity, my little man,
    You're nothing of the kind."
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Thomson -  (Bysshe Vanolis), titled "Once in a Saintly Passion", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
